meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Design with AI to Scale Your First SaaS App
content.seo_description: Break through the intermediate plateau. Use this expert checklist to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade AI applications today.
content.answer_block: Stop building 'to-do' lists and start architecting production-ready AI systems. By integrating Antigravity for rapid UI iteration and Claude Code for intelligent CLI automation, you transform from a tutorial-follower into a high-impact builder capable of shipping complex, scalable applications that solve real-world user problems effectively.
content.intro:
You’ve finished the tutorials. You know the syntax, but your projects feel like toys—brittle, unpolished, and lacking the 'wow' factor that lands internships or freelance clients. You are stuck in the intermediate purgatory, where the gap between knowing how to code and knowing how to build a product feels like an unbridgeable chasm.

It is time to change the workflow. By treating AI as your lead architect rather than a autocomplete engine, you can bypass the boilerplate and focus on product-market fit. We are moving beyond basic scripts into the realm of professional engineering, using modern tooling to iterate at the speed of thought.

steps.title: Architecting the Data Schema
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, use AI to define your data relationships and state management to prevent technical debt early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior System Architect. Design a scalable PostgreSQL schema and state management flow for a [Project Name] that requires real-time updates and AI-driven data processing. Output in Prisma schema format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create prisma/schema.prisma based on the architectural design provided.'

steps.title: Rapid UI Prototyping
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate high-fidelity UI components that match your design system, ensuring your app looks production-ready from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive, accessible dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS and Lucide icons. Include a sidebar, main content area, and a modular component for displaying AI-generated insights.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the generated dashboard layout into the src/components directory and wire up the basic navigation.'

steps.title: Implementing AI Logic
steps.description: Integrate LLM API calls using a modular service pattern to keep your codebase clean and testable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust TypeScript service layer for interacting with the OpenAI API. Include error handling, retry logic with exponential backoff, and streaming support for real-time responses.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create src/services/aiService.ts and implement the streaming logic as discussed.'

steps.title: Automating Test Suites
steps.description: Stop manually testing your app. Use Claude Code to generate unit and integration tests for every new feature you build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vitest suite for the aiService.ts file. Mock the API responses to test success, rate-limiting, and malformed JSON output scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate tests for src/services/aiService.ts and run them to ensure 90% coverage.'

steps.title: Deploying the MVP
steps.description: Configure your CI/CD pipeline to automate deployment so you can focus on building features rather than server management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs tests, builds the production bundle, and deploys to Vercel on every push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml and configure the environment variables for production.'

pro_tips: Always define a 'System Prompt' file in your repo; it acts as the source of truth for your AI's behavior across the entire app.
Use Claude Code to refactor your code into smaller modules; if a file exceeds 200 lines, ask the AI to split it by responsibility.
Never accept the first AI code output; always prompt for 'defensive programming' patterns like Zod validation for all API inputs.
Maintain a 'Decision Log' markdown file where the AI summarizes why certain architectural choices were made during the build.
Use 'dry-run' flags in your CLI commands to observe what the AI intends to change before it touches your filesystem.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, share it in our community forum to get a code review from a senior engineer and begin your first iteration cycle.
content.conversion_block: Stop dreaming about your SaaS and start coding it with the leverage of modern AI. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
